Linear's current focus is the full deployment loop — **Linear Releases** shipped in late April as a first-class CI/CD integration that tracks every issue from merge to production. Issues auto-advance through statuses as code lands in each environment, and Linear Agent can generate release notes across a version range directly from the included issues.

![Issue sidebar showing a progression of iOS releases](https://webassets.linear.app/images/ornj730p/production/ebc92422119e3015828e065d89dee22185e53a1a-3600x1760.png?q=95&auto=format&dpr=2)

**Releases.** The agent can now connect to external MCP servers (Granola, Glean, Notion, PostHog) to pull meeting notes, enterprise context, or analytics into issues and project specs. Workspace admins control access with allowlists. Separately, Linear Agent's triage automations and skills system launched in March, with Code Intelligence (codebase-aware Q&A) announced for Business and Enterprise.

**Editor improvements.** Slash commands expanded with new attachment, embed, and table primitives. Links auto-resolve to rich previews for GitHub, Figma, Loom, and Notion. The mobile editor gained drag-and-drop support for image and file uploads. Inline AI completions arrived behind a workspace setting and respect existing Privacy Mode rules.

**Customer Requests and Initiatives.** Initiatives gained read-only access for guests, and Customer Requests moved into a dedicated app surface with a unified inbox and filter set. Cycle reports got a comparison column for current vs prior cycle, and Triage now supports keyboard shortcuts for bulk reassignment.
